title: Is it appropriate to say that all humans are imprisoned in their inner experience? Circa Eve and Adam coveting leading to exile?
body:
- siloed?
- chained too?
- solitary confinement 
- is it appropriate to say that purse that is over humanity that humanity is bound to, is synonymous with being imprisoned to your inner experience? 
- while some people have advanced the argument in multiple religious traditions that humans are enslaved to their cravings or their desires, this sentence, as cosmologically and ontologically defined, is not synonymous with being imprisoned to your desires or cravings.

comments.author: joshualinog
comments.body: What about trying to liberate arrogant people? Liberate people from their inner experience? Offer people freedom and liberty from their inner experience and their (oppressively limited) perception?
